body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Newfoundland and Labrador and Prince Edward Island multiple-jurisdiction tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 Form NL428MJ calculates Newfoundland and Labrador tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.087, 0.145, 0.158, 0.178, 0.198, 0.208, 0.213, 0.218, 0.60, and 0.16. NL428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $44,192, $88,382, $157,792, $220,910, $282,214, $564,429, and $1,128,858; rates 8.7%, 14.5%, 15.8%, 17.8%, 19.8%, 20.8%, 21.3%, and 21.8%; and base amounts $0.00, $3,844.70, $10,252.25, $21,219.03, $32,454.04, $44,592.23, $103,292.95, and $223,516.33. Lines 18 to 23 add unallocated tax on split income and subtract non-refundable tax credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 60%. Lines 24 to 27 add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 60% and multiply by the NL allocation percentage. For residents, lines 29 to 36 subtract the resident adjustment based on selected Part 3 credit lines at 8.7%, dividend tax credit, and the percentage of income not allocated to NL. Lines 37 to 49 subtract provincial foreign tax credit, political contribution tax credit, direct equity tax credit, resort property investment tax credit, and venture capital tax credits. Lines 52 to 57 calculate adjusted family income. Lines 59 to 68 calculate the low-income tax reduction: $997 basic amount, $557 spouse amount, $557 eligible dependant amount, maximum $1,554, thresholds $40,460 or $23,928, and 16% reduction rate. Line 69 enters Newfoundland and Labrador tax on Part 5 line 1.

CRA T2203 2025 Form PE428MJ calculates Prince Edward Island tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.095, 0.1347, 0.166, 0.1762, 0.19, 0.575, and 0.05. PE428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $33,328, $64,656, $105,000, and $140,000; rates 9.5%, 13.47%, 16.6%, 17.62%, and 19%; and base amounts $0.00, $3,166.16, $7,386.04, $14,083.15, and $20,250.15. Lines 11 to 16 add unallocated tax on split income and subtract non-refundable tax credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 57.5%. Lines 17 to 20 add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 57.5% and multiply by the PE allocation percentage. For residents, lines 21 to 28 subtract the resident adjustment based on selected Part 3 credit lines at 9.5%, dividend tax credit, and the percentage of income not allocated to PE. Lines 30 to 35 calculate adjusted family income. Lines 37 to 52 calculate the low-income tax reduction: $350 basic reduction, $250 age reduction, $350 spouse reduction, $250 spouse age reduction, $350 eligible dependant reduction, $300 per dependent child, $22,650 base amount, 5% reduction rate, and allocation by the PE percentage. Lines 54 to 58 subtract provincial foreign tax credit and political contribution tax credit. Lines 59 to 62 subtract the equity tax credit, capped at $7,000, and enter Prince Edward Island tax on Part 5 line 2.
